items.title: Added w key in /copy to write the focused selection directly to a file, bypassing the clipboard (useful over SSH)

explanation.what_it_is: The w key in the /copy command allows you to write the currently selected text directly to a file, skipping the clipboard. This is done within the Claude Code environment.
explanation.why_it_matters: This feature is particularly useful when working over SSH, as it avoids the need to copy and paste between remote and local systems. It streamlines the workflow when you need to save selected text to a file on a remote machine.
how_to_use: Press w while in /copy mode to trigger the file write operation
Use this feature over SSH to save selections directly to remote files

items.title: Added optional description argument to /plan (e.g., /plan fix the auth bug) that enters plan mode and immediately starts

explanation.what_it_is: The /plan command now accepts an optional description argument, allowing you to enter plan mode with a specific task in mind.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update streamlines your workflow by enabling you to start planning a task immediately after invoking the command, without needing to enter a separate description step.
how_to_use: /plan fix the auth bug
/plan refactor the database schema
/plan implement new feature request

items.title: Added claude plugins as an alias for claude plugin

explanation.what_it_is: The 'claude plugins' command is now an alias for 'claude plugin', allowing users to access plugin functionality with either phrase.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change provides an alternative way to interact with plugins, making it easier for users to manage and utilize them within Claude Code.
how_to_use: Use 'claude plugins' to list and manage available plugins
Use 'claude plugins' to install or update a specific plugin

items.title: Added ExitWorktree tool to leave an EnterWorktree session

explanation.what_it_is: The ExitWorktree tool allows you to exit a session that was entered using the EnterWorktree command, returning to the previous workflow state.
explanation.why_it_matters: This addition provides a clear way to exit an EnterWorktree session, making it easier to manage workflow states and avoid potential issues caused by remaining in an unintended state.
how_to_use: Use the ExitWorktree tool to leave an EnterWorktree session when you need to return to the previous workflow state.
Invoke the ExitWorktree command to exit the current EnterWorktree session and resume normal workflow operations.

items.title: Added CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_CRON environment variable to immediately stop scheduled cron jobs mid-session

explanation.what_it_is: The CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_CRON environment variable allows you to immediately stop scheduled cron jobs in the middle of a session.
explanation.why_it_matters: This variable gives you more control over your cron jobs, enabling you to pause or stop them as needed, even after they've started running.
how_to_use: Set CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_CRON to 'true' to stop all scheduled cron jobs mid-session
Use this variable in scenarios where you need to pause or stop cron jobs due to changes in your workflow or environment

items.title: Added support for marketplace git URLs without .git suffix (Azure DevOps, AWS CodeCommit)

explanation.what_it_is: Claude Code now supports Git URLs from Azure DevOps and AWS CodeCommit that do not include the .git suffix, allowing for more flexible repository cloning and management.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update simplifies the process of working with Git repositories from these platforms, as users no longer need to manually append the .git suffix to the URL.
how_to_use: Use the updated Git URL format to clone a repository from Azure DevOps or AWS CodeCommit directly within Claude Code
Update existing repository connections to use the simplified URL format for easier management

items.title: Added /loop command to run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m check the deploy)

explanation.what_it_is: The /loop command allows you to run a prompt or slash command at a specified recurring interval, such as every 5 minutes.
explanation.why_it_matters: This enables Claude Code users to automate repetitive tasks or checks, such as monitoring deployment status, without manual intervention.
how_to_use: /loop 5m check the deploy
/loop 1h run daily report

items.title: Added cron scheduling tools for recurring prompts within a session

explanation.what_it_is: Cron scheduling tools allow you to schedule recurring prompts within a Claude Code session, enabling automated execution of tasks at specified intervals. This feature utilizes cron syntax to define the schedule.
explanation.why_it_matters: With cron scheduling, Claude Code users can automate repetitive tasks, such as data imports or report generation, to run at regular intervals without manual intervention. This streamlines workflows and reduces the need for manual prompting.
how_to_use: Use the cron scheduling tool to schedule a daily prompt to fetch and process new data
Set up a recurring prompt to generate a weekly report at a specified time
Define a cron schedule to automate a task that requires regular execution, such as cleaning up temporary files

items.title: Added voice:pushToTalk keybinding to make the voice activation key rebindable in keybindings.json (default: space) — modifier+letter combos like…

explanation.what_it_is: The voice:pushToTalk keybinding allows you to rebind the voice activation key in Claude Code, which is set to the space bar by default. This keybinding can be customized in the keybindings.json file.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update gives users more flexibility in choosing a voice activation key that suits their workflow, potentially reducing conflicts with other keyboard shortcuts. Users can now choose modifier+letter combinations as their voice activation key.
how_to_use: Open the keybindings.json file and add a custom keybinding for voice:pushToTalk, such as 'ctrl+shift+v'
Use the default space bar keybinding for voice activation
Experiment with different modifier+letter combinations to find a voice activation key that works best for your workflow

items.title: [VSCode] Added spark icon in VS Code activity bar that lists all Claude Code sessions, with sessions opening as full editors

explanation.what_it_is: A spark icon has been added to the VS Code activity bar, which lists all active Claude Code sessions. These sessions can now be opened as full editors.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update allows for easier management and access to multiple Claude Code sessions within the VS Code environment. It streamlines workflow by providing a centralized location to view and interact with all sessions.
how_to_use: Click the spark icon in the VS Code activity bar to view a list of all active Claude Code sessions.
Select a session from the list to open it as a full editor in VS Code.

items.title: [VSCode] Added full markdown document view for plans in VS Code, with support for adding comments to provide feedback

explanation.what_it_is: A full markdown document view for plans in VS Code, allowing users to view and interact with plans in a more readable format, and add comments to provide feedback.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables Claude Code users to collaborate more effectively on plans within VS Code, by providing a clear and readable view of the plan and allowing for direct feedback.
how_to_use: Open a plan in VS Code to view it in the new markdown document view
Add comments to specific sections of the plan to provide feedback to team members

items.title: [VSCode] Added native MCP server management dialog — use /mcp in the chat panel to enable/disable servers, reconnect, and manage OAuth authentication…

explanation.what_it_is: A native MCP server management dialog in VSCode that can be accessed using the /mcp command in the chat panel, allowing for server management and OAuth authentication.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update streamlines MCP server management within the VSCode environment, eliminating the need to switch between tools or interfaces to manage servers and authentication.
how_to_use: Use /mcp in the chat panel to open the server management dialog
Enable or disable servers directly from the dialog
Reconnect to servers or manage OAuth authentication using the dialog

items.title: Added the /claude-api skill for building applications with the Claude API and Anthropic SDK

explanation.what_it_is: The /claude-api skill is a new capability that allows developers to build applications using the Claude API and Anthropic SDK directly within Claude Code.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables Claude Code users to leverage the Claude API and Anthropic SDK for more complex application development, streamlining their workflow and expanding the possibilities of their projects.
how_to_use: Use the /claude-api skill to integrate Claude API functionality into custom applications
Leverage the Anthropic SDK through the /claude-api skill to enhance application capabilities

items.title: Added Ctrl+U on an empty bash prompt (!) to exit bash mode, matching escape and backspace

explanation.what_it_is: The Ctrl+U keyboard shortcut now exits bash mode when used on an empty bash prompt, similar to the existing escape and backspace keys.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change provides a new way to exit bash mode, making it easier to switch between modes and improving workflow consistency.
how_to_use: Press Ctrl+U on an empty bash prompt to exit bash mode
Use this shortcut as an alternative to escape or backspace for mode switching

items.title: Added numeric keypad support for selecting options in Claude's interview questions (previously only the number row above QWERTY worked)

explanation.what_it_is: The numeric keypad can now be used to select options in Claude's interview questions, in addition to the number row above the QWERTY keys.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change allows users to select options more conveniently, especially for those who prefer using the numeric keypad for number input.
how_to_use: Use the numeric keypad to select options in interview questions by pressing the corresponding number key.
Switch between the number row and numeric keypad interchangeably to select options.

items.title: Added optional name argument to /remote-control and claude remote-control (/remote-control My Project or --name "My Project") to set a custom session…

explanation.what_it_is: The /remote-control command and claude remote-control now accept an optional name argument, allowing you to set a custom session name. This is done by appending the name to the command, such as /remote-control My Project or using the --name flag, like --name 'My Project'.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update gives Claude Code users more control over their remote control sessions, making it easier to manage and identify multiple sessions. It simplifies workflow by allowing custom naming of sessions.
how_to_use: /remote-control My Project
claude remote-control --name 'My Project'

items.title: Added Voice STT support for 10 new languages (20 total) — Russian, Polish, Turkish, Dutch, Ukrainian, Greek, Czech, Danish, Swedish, Norwegian

explanation.what_it_is: Voice STT support has been added for 10 new languages, bringing the total to 20 supported languages, including Russian, Polish, and Turkish among others.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update allows Claude Code users to transcribe voice recordings in more languages, expanding the platform's usability for users working with multilingual audio content.
how_to_use: Use the Voice STT feature to transcribe Russian audio files
Enable Polish language support for voice-controlled applications
Test STT accuracy with Turkish voice recordings

items.title: Added effort level display (e.g., "with low effort") to the logo and spinner, making it easier to see which effort setting is active

explanation.what_it_is: The effort level display shows the currently active effort setting, such as 'with low effort', next to the logo and on the spinner.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change makes it easier for users to verify which effort setting is active, reducing potential mistakes or confusion.
how_to_use: Check the logo to quickly see the active effort setting
Verify the effort setting on the spinner before running a task

items.title: Added agent name display in terminal title when using claude --agent

explanation.what_it_is: The terminal title now displays the agent name when running Claude Code with the --agent option.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update makes it easier to identify which agent is currently in use, especially when working with multiple agents.
how_to_use: Run 'claude --agent <agent_name>' to see the agent name in the terminal title
Use this feature to quickly verify the active agent in your workflow

items.title: Added sandbox.enableWeakerNetworkIsolation setting (macOS only) to allow Go programs like gh, gcloud, and terraform to verify TLS certificates when…

explanation.what_it_is: The sandbox.enableWeakerNetworkIsolation setting allows Go programs to verify TLS certificates on macOS, bypassing stricter network isolation rules.
explanation.why_it_matters: This setting enables tools like gh, gcloud, and terraform to function correctly on macOS by verifying TLS certificates, which is essential for secure communication.
how_to_use: Set sandbox.enableWeakerNetworkIsolation to true in your Claude Code settings on macOS to enable TLS certificate verification for Go programs.
Use this setting to run tools like gh, gcloud, and terraform with verified TLS certificates.

items.title: Added includeGitInstructions setting (and CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS env var) to remove built-in commit and PR workflow instructions from…

explanation.what_it_is: The includeGitInstructions setting and CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS environment variable allow you to remove built-in commit and PR workflow instructions from Claude Code.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update matters because it gives Claude Code users more control over their workflow, allowing them to customize or disable the built-in Git instructions if they have their own workflow or use a different version control system.
how_to_use: Set includeGitInstructions to false in your Claude Code settings to remove the built-in instructions.
Use the CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS environment variable to disable the instructions for all projects.

items.title: Added /reload-plugins command to activate pending plugin changes without restarting

explanation.what_it_is: The /reload-plugins command allows you to apply pending plugin changes without having to restart Claude Code.
explanation.why_it_matters: This command saves time by eliminating the need for a full restart when updating or changing plugins, making the development workflow more efficient.
how_to_use: Run /reload-plugins in the Claude Code interface to apply pending plugin changes
Use this command after installing or updating plugins to activate their functionality without restarting

items.title: Added a one-time startup prompt suggesting Claude Code Desktop on macOS and Windows (max 3 showings, dismissible)

explanation.what_it_is: A one-time startup prompt that suggests using Claude Code Desktop on macOS and Windows, which can be dismissed by the user and will appear no more than three times.
explanation.why_it_matters: This prompt informs users about the availability of a desktop version of Claude Code, potentially streamlining their workflow by providing an alternative to the current interface.
how_to_use: Dismiss the prompt if you prefer the current interface
Click to learn more about Claude Code Desktop and its features
Install Claude Code Desktop for a potentially more integrated experience on macOS or Windows

items.title: Added ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} variable for skills to reference their own directory in SKILL.md content

explanation.what_it_is: The ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} variable allows skills to reference their own directory in SKILL.md content, enabling dynamic file paths and links.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update simplifies skill development by making it easier to create relative links and file references within SKILL.md files, reducing the need for hardcoded absolute paths.
how_to_use: Use ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} to create links to local files, such as images or documentation.
Reference ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} in SKILL.md to include dynamic content from the skill's own directory.

items.title: Added InstructionsLoaded hook event that fires when CLAUDE.md or .claude/rules/*.md files are loaded into context

explanation.what_it_is: The InstructionsLoaded hook event is a new callback that triggers when CLAUDE.md or .claude/rules/*.md files are loaded into the context, allowing for custom actions to be taken after these files are processed.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables Claude Code users to execute custom code or perform specific actions immediately after the instructions or rules files are loaded, providing more control over the workflow.
how_to_use: Use the InstructionsLoaded hook to initialize variables or setup functions that rely on the loaded instructions or rules.
Execute custom validation or formatting on the loaded files to ensure consistency or compliance with specific standards.

items.title: Added agent_id (for subagents) and agent_type (for subagents and --agent) to hook events

explanation.what_it_is: The agent_id and agent_type have been added to hook events, allowing for identification of subagents and the type of agent used, including those specified with the --agent flag.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables more detailed tracking and logging of agent interactions, particularly when working with subagents, by providing additional context in hook events.
how_to_use: Use the agent_id to distinguish between actions taken by different subagents in your workflow.
Check the agent_type in hook events to determine if an action was initiated by a subagent or the main agent specified with --agent.

items.title: Added worktree field to status line hook commands with name, path, branch, and original repo directory when running in a --worktree session

explanation.what_it_is: The worktree field is now included in status line hook commands when running Claude Code in a --worktree session, providing information on the worktree's name, path, branch, and original repository directory.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update allows Claude Code users to access worktree-specific details in their status line hook commands, enabling more informed and context-aware automation and scripting.
how_to_use: Use the worktree field in a status line hook command to display the current worktree's name and path.
Access the worktree's branch information to automate tasks based on the current branch.
Utilize the original repository directory to construct relative paths or perform directory-specific operations.

items.title: Added pluginTrustMessage in managed settings to append organization-specific context to the plugin trust warning shown before installation

explanation.what_it_is: The pluginTrustMessage setting allows administrators to add custom text to the plugin trust warning that appears before a plugin is installed, providing organization-specific context.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change enables administrators to provide additional information to users about the trustworthiness of plugins, helping to inform their installation decisions and potentially reducing security risks.
how_to_use: Configure the pluginTrustMessage setting in managed settings to display a custom warning message before plugin installation.
Use this setting to provide context about your organization's plugin validation process or security policies.

items.title: Added policy limit fetching (e.g., remote control restrictions) for Team plan OAuth users, not just Enterprise

explanation.what_it_is: Policy limit fetching allows Team plan OAuth users to retrieve restrictions such as remote control limitations, previously only available to Enterprise users.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables Team plan users to access and manage policy limits, providing more control over their environment.
how_to_use: Use the OAuth API to fetch policy limits for your Team plan account
Check remote control restrictions for your users

items.title: Added pathPattern to strictKnownMarketplaces for regex-matching file/directory marketplace sources alongside hostPattern restrictions

explanation.what_it_is: The pathPattern feature allows regex-matching of file and directory marketplace sources in strictKnownMarketplaces, in addition to existing hostPattern restrictions.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables more precise control over marketplace sources, allowing Claude Code users to filter and manage files and directories more effectively.
how_to_use: Use pathPattern to specify regex patterns for file and directory names in marketplace sources
Combine pathPattern with hostPattern restrictions for more granular control over source filtering

items.title: Added plugin source type git-subdir to point to a subdirectory within a git repo

explanation.what_it_is: The git-subdir plugin source type allows you to specify a subdirectory within a git repository as the source for a plugin, rather than the entire repository.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables more precise control over plugin sources, reducing potential clutter and making it easier to manage plugins that are stored in larger repositories.
how_to_use: Use the git-subdir type when adding a plugin source, followed by the repository URL and the path to the subdirectory containing the plugin code.
For example, 'git-subdir https://github.com/user/repo.git path/to/plugin'

items.title: Added oauth.authServerMetadataUrl config option for MCP servers to specify a custom OAuth metadata discovery URL when standard discovery fails

explanation.what_it_is: A new config option called oauth.authServerMetadataUrl that allows MCP servers to specify a custom OAuth metadata discovery URL.
explanation.why_it_matters: This option is useful when the standard OAuth metadata discovery fails, providing a fallback to ensure authentication works as expected.
how_to_use: Set oauth.authServerMetadataUrl to a custom URL in your MCP server config when standard discovery is not working.
Use this option to override the default metadata discovery URL for specific OAuth setups.

items.title: Fixed --model claude-opus-4-0 and --model claude-opus-4-1 resolving to deprecated Opus versions instead of current

explanation.what_it_is: The --model claude-opus-4-0 and --model claude-opus-4-1 flags now correctly resolve to the current Opus versions instead of deprecated ones.
explanation.why_it_matters: This fix ensures that users of these models get the latest functionality and bug fixes, rather than being stuck with outdated versions.
how_to_use: Use --model claude-opus-4-0 or --model claude-opus-4-1 to access the current Opus versions in your Claude Code workflow.

items.title: [VSCode] Added compaction display as a collapsible "Compacted chat" card with the summary inside

explanation.what_it_is: A new collapsible 'Compacted chat' card in VSCode that displays a summary of compacted chat logs
explanation.why_it_matters: This feature allows Claude Code users to quickly view a summary of compacted chat logs without having to expand the entire log, making it easier to navigate and review conversations
how_to_use: Open the Claude Code extension in VSCode to view the 'Compacted chat' card
Click on the card to expand or collapse the summary

items.title: Added /simplify and /batch bundled slash commands

explanation.what_it_is: The /simplify and /batch commands are two new bundled slash commands in Claude Code, allowing users to simplify code and process batches of data respectively.
explanation.why_it_matters: These commands simplify workflows by providing quick access to code simplification and batch processing, reducing the need for manual intervention.
how_to_use: Use /simplify to reduce complexity in your code
Use /batch to process multiple data sets at once

items.title: Added ENABLE_CLAUDEAI_MCP_SERVERS=false env var to opt out from making claude.ai MCP servers available

explanation.what_it_is: A new environment variable ENABLE_CLAUDEAI_MCP_SERVERS that can be set to false to prevent Claude AI MCP servers from being available.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update allows Claude Code users to opt out of using Claude AI MCP servers, providing more control over their environment.
how_to_use: Set ENABLE_CLAUDEAI_MCP_SERVERS=false in your environment variables to disable Claude AI MCP servers
Use this setting when you want to restrict access to Claude AI MCP servers for security or compliance reasons

items.title: Added HTTP hooks, which can POST JSON to a URL and receive JSON instead of running a shell command

explanation.what_it_is: HTTP hooks allow Claude Code to send JSON data to a specified URL and receive JSON in response, bypassing the need for shell commands.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables Claude Code users to integrate with web services and APIs directly, simplifying workflows and reducing reliance on local command-line tools.
how_to_use: Configure an HTTP hook to send JSON data to a web service for processing
Use the received JSON response to inform subsequent actions in your Claude Code workflow

items.title: Added manual URL paste fallback during MCP OAuth authentication. If the automatic localhost redirect doesn't work, you can paste the callback URL to…

explanation.what_it_is: A manual URL paste fallback for MCP OAuth authentication, allowing users to paste the callback URL when the automatic localhost redirect fails.
explanation.why_it_matters: This fallback ensures that users can still complete the OAuth authentication process even if the automatic redirect doesn't work, preventing authentication failures.
how_to_use: When the automatic localhost redirect fails, copy the callback URL from the authentication page
Paste the callback URL into the prompt to complete the authentication process

items.title: Added "Always copy full response" option to the /copy picker. When selected, future /copy commands will skip the code block picker and copy the full…

explanation.what_it_is: The 'Always copy full response' option allows you to skip the code block picker and copy the full response when using the /copy command.
explanation.why_it_matters: This option saves time by eliminating the need to select a code block every time you want to copy a full response, streamlining your workflow in Claude Code.
how_to_use: Select the 'Always copy full response' option in the /copy picker to enable this feature.
Use the /copy command to copy the full response without being prompted to select a code block.

items.title: Added /copy command to show an interactive picker when code blocks are present, allowing selection of individual code blocks or the full response.

explanation.what_it_is: The /copy command is a new feature that displays an interactive picker when code blocks are present in a response, enabling users to select either individual code blocks or the entire response for copying.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update streamlines the process of copying specific code snippets from Claude Code responses, saving users time and reducing manual effort.
how_to_use: Use the /copy command in a conversation with code blocks to open the interactive picker.
Select individual code blocks or the full response from the picker to copy them to your clipboard.

items.title: We've launched automatic caching for the Messages API
items.summary: We've launched automatic caching for the Messages API. Add a single cache_control field to your request body and the system automatically caches the last cacheable block, moving the cache point…

explanation.what_it_is: Automatic caching for the Messages API, which stores the last cacheable block of a request, allowing for faster subsequent requests.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update reduces the number of requests needed to retrieve previously fetched data, resulting in improved performance and lower latency for Claude Code users.
how_to_use: Add a cache_control field to your request body to enable automatic caching
Use the cached response to reduce the number of requests to the Messages API

items.title: We've retired the Claude Sonnet 3.7 model (claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219) and the…
items.summary: We've retired the Claude Sonnet 3.7 model (claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219) and the Claude Haiku 3.5 model (claude-3-5-haiku-20241022). All requests to these models will now return an error. We recommend…

explanation.what_it_is: The Claude Sonnet 3.7 and Claude Haiku 3.5 models have been retired, meaning they are no longer available for use.
explanation.why_it_matters: Any existing code or workflows that rely on these models will now return an error, requiring updates to use alternative models.
how_to_use: Update your API requests to use a supported model, such as the latest Claude Sonnet or Haiku versions.
Review your code for any hardcoded references to the retired models and replace them with supported alternatives.

items.title: We announced the deprecation of the Claude Haiku 3 model…
items.summary: We announced the deprecation of the Claude Haiku 3 model (claude-3-haiku-20240307), with retirement scheduled for April 19, 2026. We recommend migrating to Claude Haiku 4.5. Read more in model…

explanation.what_it_is: The Claude Haiku 3 model has been deprecated, meaning it will no longer be supported or maintained after its scheduled retirement date.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change requires users to migrate their applications to the newer Claude Haiku 4.5 model to ensure continued functionality beyond April 19, 2026.
how_to_use: Update your API requests to use the Claude Haiku 4.5 model instead of the deprecated Claude Haiku 3 model
Review the documentation for any changes in model behavior or parameters between Claude Haiku 3 and Claude Haiku 4.5

items.title: We've launched Claude Sonnet 4.6, our latest balanced model combining speed and…
items.summary: We've launched Claude Sonnet 4.6, our latest balanced model combining speed and intelligence for everyday tasks. Sonnet 4.6 delivers improved agentic search performance while consuming fewer tokens.…

explanation.what_it_is: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is a balanced model that combines speed and intelligence for everyday tasks, delivering improved agentic search performance while reducing token consumption.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update matters because it enables Claude Code users to achieve better search results with fewer resources, making it more efficient for tasks that rely on search performance.
how_to_use: Use Sonnet 4.6 for tasks that require agentic search, such as information retrieval or data lookup, to take advantage of improved performance and reduced token consumption.

items.title: API code execution is now free when used with web search or web fetch
items.summary: API code execution is now free when used with web search or web fetch. Sandboxed code execution improves model capability and token efficiency. See the pricing details for standalone usage.

explanation.what_it_is: API code execution is now free when used in conjunction with web search or web fetch, allowing for sandboxed code execution that improves model capability and token efficiency.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change reduces costs for developers who use web search or web fetch with API code execution, making it more economical to leverage these features in their applications.
how_to_use: Use API code execution with web search to fetch and process data without incurring extra costs
Combine web fetch with API code execution to retrieve and manipulate data from external sources at no additional charge

items.title: The web search tool and programmatic tool calling are now generally available…
items.summary: The web search tool and programmatic tool calling are now generally available (no beta header required). Web search and web fetch now support dynamic filtering, which uses code execution to filter…

explanation.what_it_is: The web search tool and programmatic tool calling in the Anthropic API are now out of beta and available for general use, with added support for dynamic filtering using code execution.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change allows Claude Code users to leverage web search and web fetch with more precise control over the results, enabling more accurate and relevant data retrieval in their applications.
how_to_use: Use dynamic filtering to narrow down web search results based on custom criteria
Integrate web fetch with dynamic filtering to retrieve specific data from websites

items.title: The code execution tool, web fetch tool, tool search tool, tool use examples,…
items.summary: The code execution tool, web fetch tool, tool search tool, tool use examples, and memory tool are now generally available (no beta header required).

explanation.what_it_is: The code execution, web fetch, tool search, tool use examples, and memory tools are now fully available without beta restrictions.
explanation.why_it_matters: Claude Code users can now rely on these tools for production use without worrying about beta limitations or potential changes.
how_to_use: Use the code execution tool to run code snippets directly within Claude Code
Utilize the web fetch tool to retrieve data from web APIs
Leverage the tool search tool to quickly find and access relevant tools

items.title: We've launched fast mode in research preview for Opus 4.6, providing…
items.summary: We've launched fast mode in research preview for Opus 4.6, providing significantly faster output token generation via the speed parameter. Fast mode is up to 2.5x as fast at premium pricing.…

explanation.what_it_is: Fast mode is a new feature in Opus 4.6 that generates output tokens up to 2.5x faster than standard mode, accessible via the speed parameter.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update allows Claude Code users to accelerate their workflows, particularly those that rely on rapid output generation, at a premium pricing tier.
how_to_use: Set the speed parameter to enable fast mode in your Opus 4.6 requests
Test fast mode with your existing workflows to measure the performance improvement

items.title: We've launched Claude Opus 4.6, our most intelligent model for complex agentic…
items.summary: We've launched Claude Opus 4.6, our most intelligent model for complex agentic tasks and long-horizon work. Opus 4.6 recommends adaptive thinking (thinking: {type: "adaptive"}); manual thinking…

explanation.what_it_is: Claude Opus 4.6 is a model update that enables adaptive thinking for complex tasks, allowing for more flexible and dynamic problem-solving. It can be invoked by specifying the thinking type as 'adaptive'.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update matters because it allows Claude Code users to tackle more intricate and long-term tasks, such as multi-step planning and decision-making, with improved model intelligence. Users can now leverage adaptive thinking to generate more effective and context-aware solutions.
how_to_use: Specify 'thinking: {type: 'adaptive'}' in your API requests to utilize the new adaptive thinking capabilities.
Use Opus 4.6 for tasks that require flexible and dynamic problem-solving, such as generating creative content or responding to open-ended questions.

items.title: The effort parameter is now generally available (no beta header required) and…
items.summary: The effort parameter is now generally available (no beta header required) and supports Claude Opus 4.6. Effort replaces budget_tokens for controlling thinking depth on new models.

explanation.what_it_is: The effort parameter is a control for thinking depth in Claude models, replacing budget_tokens for new models, and is now supported in Claude Opus 4.6.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change allows for more precise control over the depth of thinking in models, and simplifies the API by removing the need for the beta header and budget_tokens parameter.
how_to_use: Use the effort parameter in place of budget_tokens when calling Claude models to control thinking depth.
Update existing API calls to remove the beta header and replace budget_tokens with effort for new models.

items.title: We've launched the compaction API in beta, providing server-side context…
items.summary: We've launched the compaction API in beta, providing server-side context summarization for effectively infinite conversations. Available on Opus 4.6.

explanation.what_it_is: The compaction API is a server-side context summarization tool that enables effective conversation summarization, even for very long conversations.
explanation.why_it_matters: This API allows Claude Code users to handle conversations of virtually any length, without having to manage complex context manually.
how_to_use: Use the compaction API to summarize user input in chat applications, reducing the need for manual context management.
Integrate the compaction API with Opus 4.6 to leverage server-side context summarization in your applications.

items.title: We've introduced data residency controls, allowing you to specify where model…
items.summary: We've introduced data residency controls, allowing you to specify where model inference runs with the inference_geo parameter. US-only inference is available at 1.1x pricing for models released after…

explanation.what_it_is: Data residency controls allow you to specify the geographic location where model inference runs using the inference_geo parameter.
explanation.why_it_matters: This feature enables you to comply with data sovereignty requirements by controlling where your data is processed, with US-only inference available at a slightly higher pricing tier for newer models.
how_to_use: Use the inference_geo parameter to specify the desired geographic location for model inference, such as 'US' for US-only inference.
Check the pricing documentation for models released after the introduction of this feature to determine the 1.1x pricing tier for US-only inference.

items.title: The 1M token context window is now available in beta for Claude Opus 4.6, in…
items.summary: The 1M token context window is now available in beta for Claude Opus 4.6, in addition to Sonnet 4.5 and Sonnet 4. Long context pricing applies to requests exceeding 200K input tokens.

explanation.what_it_is: The 1M token context window is a feature that allows Claude Opus 4.6 to process and understand longer input sequences, up to 1 million tokens. This increases the amount of text that can be considered when generating responses.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change enables Claude Code users to work with larger documents or longer conversations, which can be useful for tasks like document summarization or dialogue generation. However, requests exceeding 200K input tokens will incur long context pricing.
how_to_use: Use the 1M token context window to process large documents, such as books or research papers, and generate summaries or analyses.
Apply the feature to long conversations or dialogues to improve the model's understanding of context and generate more accurate responses.

items.title: Fine-grained tool streaming is now generally available on all models and…
items.summary: Fine-grained tool streaming is now generally available on all models and platforms (no beta header required). The output_format parameter for structured outputs has been moved to output_config.format.

explanation.what_it_is: Fine-grained tool streaming is now available on all models and platforms, allowing for more precise control over output formatting. The output_format parameter has been moved to output_config.format for structured outputs.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change allows Claude Code users to have more consistent and flexible output formatting across all models and platforms. It also simplifies the configuration process by consolidating output format settings into a single location.
how_to_use: Update your API requests to use output_config.format instead of output_format for structured outputs
Test your existing workflows to ensure compatibility with the new output configuration

items.title: Structured outputs are now generally available on the Claude API for Claude…
items.summary: Structured outputs are now generally available on the Claude API for Claude Sonnet 4.5, Claude Opus 4.5, and Claude Haiku 4.5. GA includes expanded schema support, improved grammar compilation…

explanation.what_it_is: Structured outputs are now available on the Claude API, allowing for more organized and formatted responses from Claude models, including expanded schema support and improved grammar compilation.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables Claude Code users to receive more structured and readable output from the API, making it easier to integrate and process the results in their applications.
how_to_use: Use the Claude API to generate structured outputs for Claude Sonnet 4.5, Claude Opus 4.5, and Claude Haiku 4.5 models
Take advantage of expanded schema support to define custom output formats
Leverage improved grammar compilation for more accurate and consistent output

items.title: console.anthropic.com now redirects to platform.claude.com
items.summary: console.anthropic.com now redirects to platform.claude.com. The Claude Console has moved to its new home as part of our Claude brand consolidation. Existing bookmarks and links will continue working…

explanation.what_it_is: The console.anthropic.com URL now redirects to platform.claude.com, indicating a change in the location of the Claude Console as part of the Claude brand consolidation.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change affects bookmarks and links to the old URL, but existing ones will continue to work due to the redirect, ensuring minimal disruption to workflows.
how_to_use: Update bookmarks to point to platform.claude.com for direct access to the Claude Console
Verify that existing links and integrations still function as expected after the redirect

items.title: We've retired the Claude Opus 3 model (claude-3-opus-20240229)
items.summary: We've retired the Claude Opus 3 model (claude-3-opus-20240229). All requests to this model will now return an error. We recommend upgrading to Claude Opus 4.5, which offers significantly improved…

explanation.what_it_is: The Claude Opus 3 model (claude-3-opus-20240229) has been retired and is no longer available for use, with all requests to this model now returning an error.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change requires users to upgrade to Claude Opus 4.5 to continue using the service, which offers improved performance and capabilities.
how_to_use: Update your API requests to use the Claude Opus 4.5 model instead of the retired Claude Opus 3 model
Test your application with the new model to ensure compatibility and handle any potential errors

items.title: We announced the deprecation of the Claude Haiku 3.5 model
items.summary: We announced the deprecation of the Claude Haiku 3.5 model. Read more in our documentation.

explanation.what_it_is: The Claude Haiku 3.5 model is being deprecated, meaning it will no longer be supported or maintained.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change affects Claude Code users who currently rely on the Haiku 3.5 model, as they will need to migrate to a different model to ensure continued support and compatibility.
how_to_use: Review the documentation to understand the deprecation timeline and recommended replacement models
Update existing code to use a supported model, such as the latest Haiku version

items.title: Structured outputs now supports Claude Haiku 4.5
items.summary: Structured outputs now supports Claude Haiku 4.5.

explanation.what_it_is: Structured outputs in Claude Code now support the Claude Haiku 4.5 format, allowing for more organized and formatted output.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables Claude Code users to take advantage of the features and improvements in Claude Haiku 4.5, such as enhanced formatting and organization capabilities, in their structured output.
how_to_use: Use the updated structured outputs to generate more readable and formatted text in your Claude Code applications
Integrate Claude Haiku 4.5 formatted output into your existing workflows to improve output consistency and quality

items.title: We've launched Claude Opus 4.5, our most intelligent model combining maximum…
items.summary: We've launched Claude Opus 4.5, our most intelligent model combining maximum capability with practical performance. Ideal for complex specialized tasks, professional software engineering, and…

explanation.what_it_is: Claude Opus 4.5 is a new model that combines high capability with practical performance, suitable for complex and specialized tasks.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update matters because it enables Claude Code users to tackle more complex tasks, such as professional software engineering, with improved performance.
how_to_use: Use Claude Opus 4.5 for complex software engineering tasks that require high capability and practical performance.
Leverage the model's capabilities for specialized tasks that require advanced intelligence and performance.

items.title: We've launched programmatic tool calling in public beta, allowing Claude to…
items.summary: We've launched programmatic tool calling in public beta, allowing Claude to call tools from within code execution to reduce latency and token usage in multi-tool workflows.

explanation.what_it_is: Programmatic tool calling allows Claude to invoke external tools from within code execution, reducing the need for manual intervention.
explanation.why_it_matters: This feature reduces latency and token usage in workflows that involve multiple tools, making it more efficient to execute complex tasks.
how_to_use: Call a tool from within a Claude code block to fetch external data
Use programmatic tool calling to automate workflows that previously required manual tool invocation

items.title: We've launched the tool search tool in public beta, enabling Claude to…
items.summary: We've launched the tool search tool in public beta, enabling Claude to dynamically discover and load tools on-demand from large tool catalogs.

explanation.what_it_is: The tool search tool is a feature that allows Claude to dynamically discover and load tools on-demand from large tool catalogs.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables Claude to access a wider range of tools without requiring manual configuration, making it easier to integrate new tools into workflows.
how_to_use: Use the tool search tool to find and load tools from catalogs, streamlining tool discovery and integration
Leverage on-demand tool loading to reduce the need for manual tool configuration and updates

items.title: We've launched the effort parameter in public beta for Claude Opus 4.5,…
items.summary: We've launched the effort parameter in public beta for Claude Opus 4.5, allowing you to control token usage by trading off between response thoroughness and efficiency.

explanation.what_it_is: The effort parameter is a new control in Claude Opus 4.5 that allows you to balance the thoroughness of responses against the efficiency of token usage.
explanation.why_it_matters: This parameter gives Claude Code users more fine-grained control over the trade-off between response quality and computational cost, enabling more efficient use of resources.
how_to_use: Set the effort parameter to a lower value to reduce token usage and increase response speed, at the cost of potentially less thorough responses.
Set the effort parameter to a higher value to increase response thoroughness, at the cost of increased token usage and potentially slower response times.

items.title: We've added client-side compaction to our Python and TypeScript SDKs,…
items.summary: We've added client-side compaction to our Python and TypeScript SDKs, automatically managing conversation context through summarization when using tool_runner.

explanation.what_it_is: Client-side compaction is a feature in the Python and TypeScript SDKs that automatically manages conversation context by summarizing it when using the tool_runner.
explanation.why_it_matters: This feature reduces the need for manual context management, making it easier to integrate the Anthropic API into applications that rely on tool_runner.
how_to_use: Use the tool_runner with the updated SDKs to automatically summarize conversation context
Enable client-side compaction in your application to reduce context management overhead

items.title: Search result content blocks are now generally available on Amazon Bedrock
items.summary: Search result content blocks are now generally available on Amazon Bedrock. Learn more in our search results documentation.

explanation.what_it_is: Search result content blocks allow developers to embed search results directly into their applications, and this feature is now available for use on Amazon Bedrock.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables Claude Code users to integrate search functionality into their Amazon Bedrock applications, making it easier to provide relevant search results to their users.
how_to_use: Use the search result content blocks to display relevant search results within your Amazon Bedrock application
Refer to the search results documentation for implementation details and best practices

items.title: We've launched a new documentation platform at platform.claude.com/docs
items.summary: We've launched a new documentation platform at platform.claude.com/docs. Our documentation now lives side by side with the Claude Console, providing a unified developer experience. The previous docs…

explanation.what_it_is: A new documentation platform for Claude Code, hosted at platform.claude.com/docs, which unifies documentation and the Claude Console.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change streamlines the developer experience by providing a single location for documentation and console access, making it easier to find information and work with Claude Code.
how_to_use: Access the new documentation platform at platform.claude.com/docs to find information on using Claude Code
Use the unified platform to switch between documentation and the Claude Console without navigating to separate sites

items.title: We've launched Claude in Microsoft Foundry, bringing Claude models to Azure…
items.summary: We've launched Claude in Microsoft Foundry, bringing Claude models to Azure customers with Azure billing and OAuth authentication. Access the full Messages API including extended thinking, prompt…

explanation.what_it_is: Claude models are now available in Microsoft Foundry, allowing Azure customers to access the full Messages API, including extended thinking and prompt capabilities, with Azure billing and OAuth authentication.
explanation.why_it_matters: This integration enables Azure customers to leverage Claude's capabilities with the convenience of Azure billing and authentication, streamlining the development process.
how_to_use: Use Azure OAuth to authenticate and authorize access to Claude models
Access the Messages API to integrate Claude's extended thinking and prompt capabilities into Azure-based applications

items.title: We've launched structured outputs in public beta, providing guaranteed schema…
items.summary: We've launched structured outputs in public beta, providing guaranteed schema conformance for Claude's responses. Use JSON outputs for structured data responses or strict tool use for validated tool…

explanation.what_it_is: Structured outputs provide a guaranteed schema for Claude's responses, allowing for more reliable and consistent data exchange. This is achieved through JSON outputs for structured data responses.
explanation.why_it_matters: With structured outputs, Claude Code users can now rely on a consistent data format, making it easier to integrate and process responses in their applications. This enables more robust and automated workflows.
how_to_use: Use JSON outputs to receive structured data responses from Claude
Enable strict tool use for validated tool outputs with guaranteed schema conformance

items.title: We announced the deprecation of the Claude Sonnet 3.7 model
items.summary: We announced the deprecation of the Claude Sonnet 3.7 model. Read more in our documentation.

explanation.what_it_is: The Claude Sonnet 3.7 model has been deprecated, meaning it will no longer be supported or maintained.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change affects Claude Code users who rely on the 3.7 model, as they will need to migrate to a newer model to ensure continued support and compatibility.

Update existing code to use a supported Claude model version

items.title: We've retired the Claude Sonnet 3.5 models
items.summary: We've retired the Claude Sonnet 3.5 models. All requests to these models will now return an error.

explanation.what_it_is: The Claude Sonnet 3.5 models have been removed from the Anthropic API and developer platform, resulting in an error for any requests to these models.
explanation.why_it_matters: Any existing code or workflows relying on the Claude Sonnet 3.5 models will need to be updated to use alternative models to avoid errors.
how_to_use: Update API requests to use a supported model version
Test existing workflows for errors related to the retired models

items.title: We've expanded context editing with thinking block clearing…
items.summary: We've expanded context editing with thinking block clearing (clear_thinking_20251015), enabling automatic management of thinking blocks. Learn more in our context editing documentation.

explanation.what_it_is: The clear_thinking_20251015 feature allows automatic management of thinking blocks in context editing, enabling the removal of unnecessary blocks.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update simplifies the context editing process by automatically clearing thinking blocks, reducing manual effort and potential errors.
how_to_use: Use the clear_thinking_20251015 feature to automatically remove unnecessary thinking blocks in your context editing workflow.
Refer to the context editing documentation for more information on implementing this feature.

items.title: We've launched Agent Skills (skills-2025-10-02 beta), a new way to extend…
items.summary: We've launched Agent Skills (skills-2025-10-02 beta), a new way to extend Claude's capabilities. Skills are organized folders of instructions, scripts, and resources that Claude loads dynamically to…

explanation.what_it_is: Agent Skills are organized folders of instructions, scripts, and resources that Claude loads dynamically to extend its capabilities.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update allows Claude Code users to create and load custom skills, enabling more flexible and dynamic interactions with the model.
how_to_use: Create a skill folder with instructions and scripts to teach Claude a new task
Load a custom skill dynamically to extend Claude's capabilities for a specific use case

items.title: We've launched Claude Haiku 4.5, our fastest and most intelligent Haiku model…
items.summary: We've launched Claude Haiku 4.5, our fastest and most intelligent Haiku model with near-frontier performance. Ideal for real-time applications, high-volume processing, and cost-sensitive deployments…

explanation.what_it_is: Claude Haiku 4.5 is an updated Haiku model that offers near-frontier performance, making it suitable for applications requiring speed and efficiency.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update matters because it enables Claude Code users to deploy Haiku models in real-time applications, handle high-volume processing, and reduce costs in their deployments.
how_to_use: Use Claude Haiku 4.5 for real-time applications that require fast and accurate responses
Deploy Haiku 4.5 in high-volume processing workflows to take advantage of its improved performance

items.title: We've launched Claude Sonnet 4.5, our best model for complex agents and coding,…
items.summary: We've launched Claude Sonnet 4.5, our best model for complex agents and coding, with the highest intelligence across most tasks. Learn more in the models overview.

explanation.what_it_is: Claude Sonnet 4.5 is a model update that provides the highest intelligence across most tasks, particularly for complex agents and coding.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update matters because it enables Claude Code users to tackle more complex tasks with improved accuracy and efficiency.
how_to_use: Use Claude Sonnet 4.5 for coding tasks that require advanced problem-solving and critical thinking
Leverage the model's improved intelligence for complex agent-based tasks, such as simulations and decision-making

items.title: We've introduced global endpoint pricing for AWS Bedrock and Google Vertex AI
items.summary: We've introduced global endpoint pricing for AWS Bedrock and Google Vertex AI. The Claude API (1P) pricing is unaffected.

explanation.what_it_is: Global endpoint pricing is now available for AWS Bedrock and Google Vertex AI, allowing for more unified cost management across these platforms.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change simplifies cost tracking and budgeting for Claude Code users who deploy models on AWS Bedrock or Google Vertex AI, as they can now manage costs in a more centralized manner.
how_to_use: Check the updated pricing documentation to understand the costs associated with using AWS Bedrock and Google Vertex AI endpoints
Review your current deployment costs to identify potential savings or areas for optimization

items.title: We've introduced a new stop reason model_context_window_exceeded that allows…
items.summary: We've introduced a new stop reason model_context_window_exceeded that allows you to request the maximum possible tokens without calculating input size. Learn more in our handling stop reasons…

explanation.what_it_is: A new stop reason called model_context_window_exceeded that stops the model when the context window is exceeded, allowing users to request the maximum possible tokens without calculating input size.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update allows Claude Code users to make requests without having to manually calculate the input size, simplifying the workflow and reducing potential errors.
how_to_use: Request the maximum possible tokens without specifying input size
Handle the model_context_window_exceeded stop reason in your error handling code

items.title: We've launched the memory tool in beta, enabling Claude to store and consult…
items.summary: We've launched the memory tool in beta, enabling Claude to store and consult information across conversations. Learn more in our memory tool documentation.

explanation.what_it_is: The memory tool is a beta feature that allows Claude to store and retrieve information across multiple conversations.
explanation.why_it_matters: This feature enables Claude to maintain context and provide more accurate responses based on previous interactions, changing how users can engage with Claude in multi-turn conversations.
how_to_use: Use the memory tool to store user preferences or previous conversation history
Consult the memory tool documentation to learn how to implement and utilize this feature in your application

items.title: We've launched context editing in beta, providing strategies to automatically…
items.summary: We've launched context editing in beta, providing strategies to automatically manage conversation context. The initial release supports clearing older tool results and calls when approaching token…

explanation.what_it_is: Context editing is a beta feature that automatically manages conversation context by clearing older tool results and calls when approaching token limits.
explanation.why_it_matters: This feature helps prevent context overflow and allows for more efficient conversation management, particularly when working with large or complex conversations.
how_to_use: Use context editing to clear older tool results and calls when approaching token limits
Implement strategies to automatically manage conversation context and prevent context overflow

items.title: We've launched tool helpers in beta for the Python and TypeScript SDKs,…
items.summary: We've launched tool helpers in beta for the Python and TypeScript SDKs, simplifying tool creation and execution with type-safe input validation and a tool runner for automated tool handling in…

explanation.what_it_is: Tool helpers are a new beta feature in the Python and TypeScript SDKs that provide type-safe input validation and a tool runner for automated tool handling.
explanation.why_it_matters: This simplifies the process of creating and executing tools, making it easier for developers to automate tasks and validate user input.
how_to_use: Use the tool helpers to create tools with validated input parameters
Run automated tools using the tool runner in your Python or TypeScript application

items.title: We've unified our developer offerings under the Claude brand
items.summary: We've unified our developer offerings under the Claude brand. You should see updated naming and URLs across our platform and documentation, but our developer interfaces will remain the same. Here are…

explanation.what_it_is: The developer offerings are now unified under the Claude brand, resulting in updated naming and URLs across the platform and documentation.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change simplifies the developer experience by providing a consistent brand identity, but does not affect the existing developer interfaces.
how_to_use: Update bookmarks and links to reflect the new Claude branding and URLs
Verify that existing API integrations and code continue to work as expected

items.title: We've launched the web fetch tool in beta, allowing Claude to retrieve full…
items.summary: We've launched the web fetch tool in beta, allowing Claude to retrieve full content from specified web pages and PDF documents. Learn more in our web fetch tool documentation.

explanation.what_it_is: The web fetch tool is a beta feature that enables Claude to retrieve full content from specified web pages and PDF documents.
explanation.why_it_matters: This update allows Claude Code users to access and process web-based content directly, expanding the scope of potential inputs for their applications.
how_to_use: Use the web fetch tool to retrieve HTML content from a webpage for text analysis or processing
Fetch PDF documents to extract and utilize their content in Claude-based workflows

items.title: We've launched the Claude Code Analytics API, enabling organizations to…
items.summary: We've launched the Claude Code Analytics API, enabling organizations to programmatically access daily aggregated usage metrics for Claude Code, including productivity metrics, tool usage statistics,…

explanation.what_it_is: The Claude Code Analytics API provides daily aggregated usage metrics, including productivity metrics and tool usage statistics, allowing organizations to access this data programmatically.
explanation.why_it_matters: This API enables organizations to track and analyze their Claude Code usage in a more automated and scalable way, potentially informing decisions about tool adoption and workflow optimization.
how_to_use: Use the API to fetch daily usage metrics for your organization and integrate them into your internal analytics dashboards.
Automate the generation of weekly or monthly reports on Claude Code tool usage and productivity metrics.

items.title: We launched a beta version of the CSDK
items.summary: We launched a beta version of the CSDK.

explanation.why_it_matters: This beta release provides developers with early access to the CSDK, enabling them to start building custom integrations and testing its capabilities before the full release.

Test and provide feedback on the CSDK beta to shape its development

items.title: We've launched rate limit charts in the Console Usage page, allowing you to…
items.summary: We've launched rate limit charts in the Console Usage page, allowing you to monitor your API rate limit usage and caching rates over time.

explanation.what_it_is: Rate limit charts in the Console Usage page that display API rate limit usage and caching rates over time.
explanation.why_it_matters: This allows you to monitor and track your API usage patterns, helping you identify potential rate limit issues before they occur.
how_to_use: Check the Console Usage page to view your current API rate limit usage
Use the charts to identify trends and patterns in your API usage over time

items.title: We've launched support for citable documents in client-side tool results
items.summary: We've launched support for citable documents in client-side tool results. Learn more in our tool use documentation.

Reference the tool use documentation for implementation details and best practices

items.title: We've launched v2 of the Code Execution Tool in public beta, replacing the…
items.summary: We've launched v2 of the Code Execution Tool in public beta, replacing the original Python-only tool with Bash command execution and direct file manipulation capabilities, including writing code in…

Manipulate files directly from within the Code Execution Tool

items.summary: We launched a beta version of the PHP SDK.

items.title: We've increased rate limits on the 1M token context window for Claude Sonnet 4…
items.summary: We've increased rate limits on the 1M token context window for Claude Sonnet 4 on the Claude API. For more information, see Long context rate limits.

explanation.what_it_is: The rate limits for the 1M token context window on Claude Sonnet 4 have been increased, allowing for more requests to be made within a given time frame.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change enables Claude Code users to process larger volumes of text data without hitting rate limits as frequently, which can speed up development and testing workflows.

Process longer texts or larger datasets without reducing request frequency

items.title: The 1m token context window is now available on Google Cloud's Vertex AI
items.summary: The 1m token context window is now available on Google Cloud's Vertex AI. For more information, see Claude on Vertex AI.

explanation.why_it_matters: This update enables Claude Code users to work with longer documents or more extensive text datasets on Vertex AI, potentially improving the accuracy of their models and applications.

Use the 1m token context window to process and analyze larger documents, such as books or research papers

items.title: Request IDs are now included directly in error response bodies alongside the…
items.summary: Request IDs are now included directly in error response bodies alongside the existing request-id header. Learn more in our error documentation.

explanation.what_it_is: Request IDs are now included in error response bodies, in addition to the existing request-id header.
explanation.why_it_matters: This change allows for easier error tracking and debugging, as the request ID is now directly accessible in the error response.
how_to_use: Access the request ID in error responses to identify specific failed requests
Use the included request ID to look up detailed error information in the error documentation
